item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I was so so meh on the thing, but so so loved the incident report! +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I love me some biological horror and the plant-based element is fun but it does remind me far too much of a few SciFi stories that have done it better. In particular the Warhammer 40k Tyranid comparison above is spot on. Due to my love of bio nasties it's a no vote but pretty close to a -1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Dry ice seems an odd choice for containment. If you want to deep freeze it liquid nitrogen vat would be more effective and easier to maintain. Dry ice block will sublimate away and not sure how you'd 'refresh it'

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Iceland… Yggdrasil?

item.title: Re: Staff Post - Wikiwalk Proposal
item.content:encoded: Reverted.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: If I had to guess I'd bet it was an unintended aspect of the Wikiwalk edit. Though the idea that they're so adamant about not introducing water etc to the silo that they have to say it twice is charming.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
At no time should water, water vapor, organic tissue, or visible light (with a frequency between 400-790 THz) be introduced into Silo-4. Outside of containment, SCP-1262 is extremely lethal in direct contact to organisms and should only be approached after being fully suppressed back into a dormant state.

Why was this paragraph repeated twice in the containment procedures?

item.title: Staff Post - Wikiwalk Proposal
item.content:encoded:
Timer Expired. Edits made as discussed below. Author may revert at any time.

The WikiWalk team is proposing edits to this page. All proposed edits will be in a collapsible below this post, and after 7 days if there are no serious objections to the edits, the feedback received will be taken into account as the edits are made. If you have questions not directly related to the article about this project, please pm a member of the WikiWalk Team

+ Show Block
- Hide Block

Okay, two things here. First off is linking the mention of MTF Theta-4 to its MTF hub. Additionally, the fact that this plant it able to develop defense mechanisms and offensive weapons systems reminds me strongly of SCP-831 and its likely that the SCPF would not want one getting their hands on the other. As such, I am recommending the following edits:

Changing this:

In the event of a catastrophic failure of the primary containment system or a breach in the cube itself, 6 pneumatic drills will extend from the walls and penetrate each face of the cube to deliver a powerful synthetic auxin (2,4,5-T) directly into SCP-1262. If this secondary system fails, 50% of Silo-4 will be filled with boiling paraquat and SCP-1262 will be released into the liquid. If this tertiary system fails, a series of controlled incendiary explosions using a combination of thermite, napalm, white phosphorus, and TNT will be used to suppress SCP-1262. In any case, Mobile Task Force Theta-4 (aka "Gardeners") will respond and remain onsite to provide additional security during the reestablishing/reconstruction of the containment system(s).

At no time should water, water vapor, organic tissue, or visible light (with a frequency between 400-790 THz) be introduced into Silo-4. Outside of containment, SCP-1262 is extremely lethal in direct contact to organisms and should only be approached after being fully suppressed back into a dormant state.

To this:

In the event of a catastrophic failure of the primary containment system or a breach in the cube itself, 6 pneumatic drills will extend from the walls and penetrate each face of the cube to deliver a powerful synthetic auxin (2,4,5-T) directly into SCP-1262. If this secondary system fails, 50% of Silo-4 will be filled with boiling paraquat and SCP-1262 will be released into the liquid. If this tertiary system fails, a series of controlled incendiary explosions using a combination of thermite, napalm, white phosphorus, and TNT will be used to suppress SCP-1262. In any case, Mobile Task Force Theta-4 (aka "Gardeners") will respond and remain onsite to provide additional security during the reestablishing/reconstruction of the containment system(s).

At no time should water, water vapor, organic tissue, or visible light (with a frequency between 400-790 THz) be introduced into Silo-4. Outside of containment, SCP-1262 is extremely lethal in direct contact to organisms and should only be approached after being fully suppressed back into a dormant state.

Due to potential dangers associated with cross-contamination, at no point are SCP-831 colonies to be contained at the same facility as SCP-1262.

If anyone has other/better examples that could be used here please leave a comment saying so!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
One or several images in this article have been removed due to non-compliance with the Creative Commons license. They may be added once again if/when proper attribution or proper use under CC guidelines is confirmed. Please contact apoplexic or another member of the Image Team if you have questions.


This automated message was sent on behalf of apoplexic. Please do not reply to this message or send other messages to this bot. Contact apoplexic in regard to this message. If you believe that you are not the intended recipient of this message, that the image was delivered incorrectly, or have other concerns about this bot, please contact anqxyr.

item.title: Random Article Review #1, 4 October 2016
item.content:encoded:
This is an interesting time capsule in a few respects. Reading this over, I remember how the particulars of containment procedures used to receive far more attention than they do now, both from writers and readers. We've moved away largely from that level of detail, which is probably more efficient from a storytelling perspective but was sort of a charming Series II hallmark. I may feel differently after going through more Series II articles again.

I don't usually hold with "execution can save anything" as a rule, but this is a pretty good example of a thoughtful, detailed writeup helping to create immersion and sell an article to a reader. I was interested as I read, which is really the primary yardstick. More importantly, this didn't overstay its welcome with further addenda when it very easily could have. A good blast from the middle past.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I enjoyed this article, sometimes its best to forget complex concepts and just enjoy the giant firework show this was.

Minor nitpick:

combination of thermite, napalm, white phosphorus, and TNT will be used to suppress SCP-1262.

This seems unnecessary. Stick to thermite for fast, strong burns and napalm because it adheres to the thing and cant be put out with fire, but TNT and white phosphorus would'nt be required after you annihilated something with substances far more powerful.

item.title: Actual critique
item.content:encoded:
I had originally downvoted this as the description made it sound rather over the top "keterest thing that cannot be stopped without extreme measures" and went on with details that weren't particularly interesting.

But then we got that log and all was well. Giant monster fights are fun.

item.title: Tag changes
item.content:encoded: Since that bit got the axe, removed extraterrestrial. Added plant, but not sure if that counts when technically it's a weird thing made out of plant matter rather than strictly speaking a plant.

item.title: Staff Post: Reversion Notice
item.content:encoded: Version 22 of this article has been reverted, as it made unnecessary content edits without author permission.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The containment procedures were excellent. The redundancies and excess really gave me a tense, "oh god this thing is a bad mamajamma" feeling. I'm not saying the description was bad, per se, just saying the containment procedures in this are great. Great foreshadowing, great way to present the scale of the threat.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I changed Valkyries from Omega-15 to Sigma-9 to match the new MTF page.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: From one user to another: sigs are a no-go on this forum. :)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
-1 from me. Two reasons:

1. It's too similiar to zerg/tyranid structures from Starcraft/Warhammer 40.000 respectively (capillary towers, spore colony, etc.)
2. This SCP is attempt to explain Tsunami in Indonesia erupting of Eyjafjallajökull.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: every third keter scp seems to deal with plants

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: So it's basically an alien Terra-forming machine?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
1) Is the thing making matter from out of nothing? Or bringing matter in via a wormhole or something? Because if not, the rate of growth would be limited by how quickly it could absorb carbon, and even if it was eating a bunch of animals it wouldn't get enough carbon to grow as fast as it did.

1b) If it is dependent on an external source of carbon, containment should keep it away from all sources of carbon, including the carbon in the dry ice.

2) Why is an alien form of plant life vulnerable to Terrestrial herbicides?

2b) It's vulnerability to the herbicide is only implied in the containment section, and not stated in the description section.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Hey! Sorry about that, please dont think that im ignoring your critique. I just NOW reviewed what you put in the sand box and WOW it is greatly appreciated i assure you. Give me 2 days to put it all back together with your inputs and i will mesage you when i complete it. Im typing from a mobile device in the airport so plz excuse the writing. Keep in touch!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Well, you've seen my comments on this already, so I'll spare you the details: there's a fair bit of science fail here, enough to break my suspension of disbelief. Sorry — -1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Sorry, been away for a bit. Added in paraquat and name. My bad.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: You can't just refer to a substance as "bipyridinium dichloride". For a situation like this, I assume you mean Paraquat, whose chemical name is N,N′-dimethyl-4,4′-bipyridinium dichloride. Substituents and locants are important.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Clearly the aliens just want to turn the Earth into a giant marijuana plantation.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Author may have forgotten to edit that in. Author! Give your baby a name!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Am I the only one whose bothered by the fact that this doesn't have a name on the main list?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I know azzleflux got it, and i'm not sure if anyone else caught this, but this is referring to the volcano eruption in Iceland a couple of years ago that shut down air travel in the region for a few days, and is playing that off as a cover story. I think that's pretty clever. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm confused by the timeline. Is the idea that SCP-1262 was thought completely destroyed at the conclusion of the 2010 episode, but one or more years later found to have survived? Or was that a separate instance of SCP-1262?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Hooray for good execution!

And A MILLION BONUS POINTS! You spelled Eyjafjallajökull correctly.
edit: <3

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I kind of love this— it's like a B movie monster.

These structures are supported by hardened cellulose plating formed in a hexagonal pattern. These structures also include defensive/offensive biological mechanisms that range anywhere from pneumatically discharged calcium carbonate flechettes to hallucinogenic fume releasing pods.

This is where I upvoted. The containment procedures, which were SUPER SAFE bordering on ridiculous, were a plus, too.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Doesn't really grab me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This was where I really got interested too. I like this just enough to upvote, but I'd be more excited if you went into more detail about the adaptations the thing makes. Perhaps as the infestation continues it could diverge more and more from its initial plant-like appearance?

This is really well written and I think has the potential to be a lot more than it is right now. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is kinda okay. Part of me is going "it's just a 682 tree!" and the rest is waiting for some kind of interesting hook. AetherialSkies has a good point about the terraforming. This article just feels like it needs something more, like there was going to be something after the incident log that never showed up.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Look at me, making corrections like a boss.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I would have enjoyed it if it wasn't explicitly stated that it was terraforming. If it had just sort of hinted at it, like releasing different gases, being made of weird proteins, altering the global temperature, that would have nailed me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
So…should I throw out the entire addendum and pictures then? Would that help?

Edit: Screw it…never was really in love with these parts anyway. Photos and addendum deleted.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Decent idea, until the alien-terraforming conceit. Tone/grammar needs work, pictures at the bottom aren't really needed at all.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I thought it was pretty cool until "ALIENS DID IT 1!!11!". That to mr was an incredibly lame cop out. Also I don't think the pictures at the nissan bottom were at all necessary.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The cube is to be suspended inside Silo-4 and be filled with argon gas.

I know what you meant, but this technically is saying that the ice cube should be somehow filled with argon.

SCP-1262 also has an innate resistance to most types of radiation, extreme temperatures, and extreme pressures.

But in the very next sentence.

However, extreme temperature (>1,470 degrees Kelvin or <213 degrees Kelvin) seems to be the most effective suppression method.

It's not actually a contradiction, but it seems like one at a glance. "It's fire proof, kill it with fire."

That didn't detract from my enjoyment, you tell a good story here. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I liked the multitude of precautions taken against it for its containment. It really builds up how important it is to not let it break free under any circumstances.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Not bad,but "Iceland Incident" seems a bit unclinical. Something like "Incident 1262-A" would be better IMO.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Please don't mess with the tags unless you know what you're doing.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Very detailed, but at the end of the day this is a straight-up plant monster.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I like it. I found it kind of boring up until

Inside these structures, SCP-1262 is able to produce many independently functioning organic systems for capturing and converting the ambient environment for terraforming on a planetary scale.

And then my interest was piqued. Good job.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I remember this. +1

EDIT: Tho I'm fairly certain that "Icland" is not an appropriate tag.
